title: asperula cynanchica subsp. rupicola
intro: Asperula cynanchica subsp. rupicola, also known as mountain woodruff or rocky woodruff, is a subspecies of the Asperula cynanchica plant, a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Asperula cynanchica subsp. rupicola include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
